mnemonics: PHAT: P-value, Hypothesis, Alpha, Test Statistic - the four key ingredients for any hypothesis test.
keyPoints: A hypothesis test evaluates two mutually exclusive statements (H0 and H1) about a population using sample data.
The p-value is the probability of observing your data (or something more extreme) if the null hypothesis (H0) is true.
A result is statistically significant if the p-value is less than the predetermined significance level (alpha), leading to the rejection of H0.
commonMistakes: Concluding a 'significant' result proves the alternative hypothesis is true; it only indicates the data is unlikely under H0.
Misinterpreting the p-value as the probability that the null hypothesis is true, which it is not.
